channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Four years of war have transformed Russia, but few will speak in public against it
item.description: Russia's full-scale war in Ukraine recently crossed the four-year mark. Throughout that time, one of the biggest questions has been: Is this what Russians want?
item.content:encoded: Russia's full-scale war in Ukraine recently crossed the four-year mark. Throughout that time, one of the biggest questions has been: Is this what Russians want?
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: Iran war latest: Hegseth says today marks heaviest bombing yet
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says Tuesday marks the most intense U.S. bombing yet in the Iran war. The 11 days of fighting have spooked oil markets, and the Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed.
item.content:encoded: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says Tuesday marks the most intense U.S. bombing yet in the Iran war. The 11 days of fighting have spooked oil markets, and the Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Why it took 60 years for a Vietnam vet to get the Medal of Honor
item.description: Paris Davis' was among the first Black men to lead green berets. His service in Vietnam would earn him the medal of honor, only it took nearly 60 years for him to receive the award.
item.content:encoded: Paris Davis' was among the first Black men to lead green berets. His service in Vietnam would earn him the medal of honor, only it took nearly 60 years for him to receive the award.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence

item.title: Why Russia is assisting Iran's military
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Nicole Grajewski, professor at Sciences Po and author of Russia and Iran, about Russia's reported support of Iran's military.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Nicole Grajewski, professor at Sciences Po and author of Russia and Iran, about Russia's reported support of Iran's military.
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: In Iran, sending a message outside the country is a dangerous task
item.description: Iran's internet and phone calls are still blocked, and trying to reach the outside world comes with real risk.
item.content:encoded: Iran's internet and phone calls are still blocked, and trying to reach the outside world comes with real risk.
item.dc:creator: Durrie Bouscaren

item.title: A woman witnessed a heroic act while suffering in an emergency room
item.description: On this week's "My Unsung Hero" from Hidden Brain, one woman says she witnessed a heroic act while suffering in an emergency room.
item.content:encoded: On this week's "My Unsung Hero" from Hidden Brain, one woman says she witnessed a heroic act while suffering in an emergency room.

item.title: Young Texas Muslims under political pressure find solace in Ramadan and community
item.description: Texas Republicans have invoked fear of what they call "sharia law" in rhetoric and legal actions against Islamic groups. For many young Muslims, that's at odds with the welcoming Texas they call home.
item.content:encoded: Texas Republicans have invoked fear of what they call "sharia law" in rhetoric and legal actions against Islamic groups. For many young Muslims, that's at odds with the welcoming Texas they call home.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Weber

item.title: These dancers have found the perfect rehearsal space: a train station
item.description: While commuters race through New York's Moynihan Station, dancers rehearse all down the corridor before they film their performances for social media.
item.content:encoded: While commuters race through New York's Moynihan Station, dancers rehearse all down the corridor before they film their performances for social media.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Lunden

item.title: Seeing the need, more cities and states are stepping up to help provide child care
item.description: New York City isn't the only place pushing to expand free child care. We check in on this growing trend, and how states and cities are paying for it.
item.content:encoded: New York City isn't the only place pushing to expand free child care. We check in on this growing trend, and how states and cities are paying for it.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: How Pennsylvania voters feel about Trump's claims of election fraud
item.description: President Trump recently said the only way Democrats "can get elected is to cheat, and we're going to stop it." NPR traveled through swing districts in Pennsylvania to see what people think of that.
item.content:encoded: President Trump recently said the only way Democrats "can get elected is to cheat, and we're going to stop it." NPR traveled through swing districts in Pennsylvania to see what people think of that.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt
